key_insight: The paper introduces the Patnaik-Pearson dimension, a new intrinsic-dimension estimator for neural-network representations that connects geometric structure with spectral power-law behavior.
engineering_relevance: It offers a tool for probing how token embeddings and hidden states change across layers in transformers, which can help diagnose representation compression, over-parameterization, and feature evolution.
method: Defines a new intrinsic-dimension measure for data manifolds inspired by TwoNN, HTSR, and SETOL.
Proves theoretical properties of the estimator and studies how it transforms under neural-network-style operations.
Compares the dimension estimate to Pareto-tail behavior of weight-matrix spectra and applies it to BERT-base and DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports both theoretical analysis and numerical experiments on transformer models, tracking the intrinsic dimension of token embeddings at initialization and across layers.
watch_item: The method’s interpretation may depend on assumptions such as Pareto-like empirical spectral densities, so results may be less direct when spectra deviate from a power law.
paper_title: Patnaik-Pearson intrinsic dimension for internal representations of neural networks
